attributes.action: Petition Description: Digital Privacy is a Human Right  Protect Our Future
attributes.additional_details: Privacy is for the people always. A free society is built upon this right. The government says if you have nothing to hide you have nothing to fear. This logic is completely backwards. We should never have to justify wanting to keep our lives private. Instead the state must justify spying on us. Stripping away our encryption by blocking Apple Advanced Data Protection leaves young people vulnerable to hackers. Giving all our NHS data to Palantir is reckless. We demand privacy for once!
attributes.background: Privacy is a human right. As a youth, I do not feel safe in the UK. The government wants total control of our data. They are banning Apple Advanced Data Protection and breaking encryption, while handing private NHS data to surveillance firms like Palantir. We demand our right to digital safety.

rejection.details:
Petitions need to call on the Government or Parliament to take a specific action.

We're not sure exactly what you'd like the Government or Parliament to do.

You could start a new petition explaining clearly what you would like the Government or Parliament to do.
